topNews.title: AWS engineer reports PostgreSQL perf halved by Linux 7.0, fix may not be easy
topNews.summary:
• What happened: An AWS engineer reported that PostgreSQL performance has been significantly reduced by Linux 7.0.  
• Why it matters: This performance drop could impact cloud services relying on PostgreSQL, affecting user experience and operational efficiency.  
• Business opportunity: Companies may seek alternative solutions or optimizations to maintain performance, creating demand for specialized consulting and support services.

topNews.title: I used AI. It worked. I hated it
topNews.summary:
• What happened: A user shared their experience of successfully using AI but expressed dissatisfaction with the process.  
• Why it matters: This highlights the potential disconnect between AI effectiveness and user satisfaction.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can focus on improving user experience and satisfaction in AI applications.

topNews.title: Show HN: sllm – Split a GPU node with other developers, unlimited tokens
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Running DeepSeek V3 (685B) requires 8×H100 GPUs which is about $14k/month. Most developers only need 15-25 tok/s. sllm lets you join a cohort of developers sharing a dedicated node. You reserve a spot with your card, and nobody is charged until the cohort fills. Prices start at $5/mo …
topNews.summary:
• What happened: sllm allows developers to share a GPU node, reducing costs for accessing high-demand resources.  
• Why it matters: This model democratizes access to expensive GPU resources, making it more affordable for smaller developers.  
• Business opportunity: There is potential for growth in collaborative tech solutions that lower costs for niche markets in AI development.

topNews.title: Show HN: Ray – an open-source AI financial advisor that runs in your terminal
topNews.rawTextSnippet: I've been using this daily for 4 months and figured others might find it useful. This is my first open source project so would love any feedback. Ray connects to your bank via Plaid, stores everything in an encrypted local SQLite database, and lets you ask questions about your finances in natur…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: An open-source AI financial advisor named Ray has been launched, allowing users to manage finances via terminal.  
• Why it matters: Ray enhances personal finance management by providing secure, real-time insights directly from users' bank data.  
• Business opportunity: There is potential for monetization through premium features or integrations with financial institutions and services.

topNews.title: Show HN: Sshifu – Give SSH access in 1 command. Teleport/Smallstep alternative
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Hey HN! Over the past few years, I’ve spent way too much time copy-pasting SSH public keys just to give people access to servers. It always felt like a chore, and the security risks (offboarding, revocation, etc.) aren’t great either. I looked into solutions like Teleport and Smallstep, but they fel…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: A new tool called Sshifu simplifies granting SSH access with a single command.  
• Why it matters: It addresses security risks and inefficiencies associated with managing SSH keys.  
• Business opportunity: There is potential for Sshifu to capture market share among companies seeking streamlined and secure access management solutions.

dailyBrief: In today's AI landscape, a report from an AWS engineer reveals that PostgreSQL performance has been halved following the transition to Linux 7.0, raising concerns for businesses reliant on database efficiency, with potential fixes expected to be complex and time-consuming. Meanwhile, a personal account highlights the mixed feelings surrounding AI adoption, emphasizing that while AI can deliver results, user experience remains a critical factor for acceptance. In the venture capital arena, discussions around a potential mega IPO involving SpaceX and OpenAI have sparked skepticism, with some labeling it a "grift," which could impact investor confidence in AI startups. Additionally, the launch of sllm, a tool allowing developers to share GPU resources, could democratize access to AI development, while legal troubles continue for OpenAI's CEO, Sam Altman, as a lawsuit from his sister alleging sexual abuse has been amended, potentially affecting the company's public image and leadership stability.
